commit.message:
Fix i18n config returning null in Vite dev SSR (#319)

* Fix i18n config returning null in Vite dev SSR

The admin manifest endpoint was returning i18n: null in Vite dev mode
due to a module singleton mismatch between static and dynamic imports.

In getManifest(), the i18n config was loaded via dynamic import(),
but the middleware that calls setI18nConfig() uses a static import.
In Vite dev SSR, these resolve to different module instances.

The fix reads i18n config directly from the virtual module
(virtual:emdash/config) which contains the serialized config data
populated by the Astro integration at build time.

Fixes #294

* Update packages/core/src/emdash-runtime.ts

commit.message:
feat(scanner): add .NET/MSBuild manifest scanning with CPM support

- Add dotnet.go: token-based XML parser for .csproj/.vbproj/.fsproj/.sln/.slnx
- Collect <PackageReference>/<PackageVersion> at any nesting depth, including
  inside <Choose>/<When> conditional blocks (struct-based xml.Unmarshal silently
  skipped those entire subtrees)
- Trim whitespace from child <Version> elements (encoding/xml preserves
  indentation; version strings like "\n    3.1.1\n  " are now correctly trimmed)
- Resolve unversioned PackageReference entries via Central Package Management:
  walk upward from the project directory to find Directory.Packages.props,
  look up versions case-insensitively (NuGet IDs are case-insensitive)
- Use filepath.Abs before the CPM upward walk so relative paths don't
  silently stop at the working directory instead of climbing to a parent
- Return an error (rather than silently walking further up) when
  Directory.Packages.props exists but is malformed
- Detect embedded MSBuild expressions (strings.Contains) not just prefix
  matches, so versions like 1.0.$(BuildNumber) are correctly skipped
- Deduplicate within a file: explicit in-project versions win over CPM-resolved
  ones; duplicate names are deduplicated case-insensitively
- Add path-traversal comment to collectProjectPackages explaining why
  PathSeparator is appended to both sides of the prefix check
- Add 4 new tests: whitespace trimming, Choose/When conditional blocks,
  Central Package Management resolution, ScanDirectory integration path

commit.message:
fix(solvers): add CPU fallback for OOM during linalg.solve

When the 30k×30k normal-equation solve exceeds GPU memory,
transparently fall back to CPU for the factorization and move
the result back to the original device.  Applied to all three
solver paths: tikhonov, normal_equations (Cholesky), and
solve_from_normal_equations, plus StreamingNEFClassifier.solve().

The solve is a one-shot operation so the CPU overhead is minimal
compared to re-running the entire benchmark.

New files:

  data format, figure panel mapping
- mutualism.md: full analysis of partner choice (P) at given=1.0

Key findings:
- Cross-benefit payoffs: each population's R-P equals the partner's b-c
- Pop_0 (lower b-c) cooperates in 97% of off-diagonal cells because it
  receives larger benefits from its partner
- Partner choice bottleneck: cooperation scales with min(b_c_0, b_c_1)
  because swaps require C1P1 on both sides
- Diagonal recovers Hamilton's stochastic symmetry breaking
- Genotype composition and exploitation patterns match Hamilton

feat: zero-based app shell and visual system redesign (PR1)

## What changed

### styles.py — Complete visual system rewrite
- New color palette: GitHub Primer-inspired neutral cool-gray (replacing Slate/Indigo)
  - Dark: #0D1117 bg, #58A6FF accent, #E6EDF3 text
  - Light: #FFFFFF bg, #0969DA accent, #1F2328 text
- New button variants: #Flat (ghost/toolbar), #Secondary:checked (toggle state)
- New toolbar strip styling (#ToolbarStrip): flat, bottom-border only
- New status chips (#StatusChip, #StatusChipActive): compact inline badges
- Lighter containers: subtle bg tint, thinner borders (no heavy QFrame#Container)
- Thinner progress bars (4px default, 20px #ProgressFull)
- Smaller scrollbars (8px), transparent track backgrounds
- Modern tab styling (underline-style, not bordered)
- Menu bar and menu item styling
- Reduced font sizes (body 14px, subheader 16px)
- Kept Spacing/Radius token values for API compatibility

### main_window.py — App shell restructured
- Removed header section (title bar + badge row)
- Replaced QFrame#Container control panel with flat #ToolbarStrip
- Moved badges into toolbar as compact StatusChips (hidden when no data)
- Primary action (CSV open) uses default button style; mode/tool buttons use #Flat
- Replaced boxed progress container with thin inline progress strip
- Removed QFrame#Container wrapping from graph area (borderless)
- Simplified empty state: clear title + one-line text + CTA button
- Graph:table splitter ratio 3:1 (was 2:1)
- Table set to read-only (NoEditTriggers)
- Table grid hidden, alternating row colors enabled
- Splitter handle width 6px (was 8px in old, 2px in original)

## Design rationale
The previous UI stacked header → boxed control panel → boxed progress → boxed
graph, creating a heavy "legacy Qt utility" appearance. This redesign:
1. Maximizes content area by removing chrome
2. Uses flat toolbar strip instead of bordered control panel
3. Applies calm neutral palette (GitHub Primer) instead of cold Slate/Indigo
4. Shows status only when meaningful (badges hidden until data loaded)
5. Reduces visual noise from borders, badges, and container wrapping

## Testing
- All 236 tests pass
- Lint clean (ruff check + format)
- API compatibility maintained: Spacing/Radius values unchanged, Colors API same

* Fix noisy audio: rebuild Windows DLL with libvgmstream_setup PCM16 output

Previous commit bb7ea19 fixed Linux .so but missed the Windows .dll.
The user was running the old Windows DLL which output float32 (4 bytes/sample),
causing noise when Godot's AudioStreamWAV FORMAT_16_BITS tried to interpret
float bytes as int16.

Both binaries now rebuilt with libvgmstream_setup(force_sfmt=PCM16):
- libgodot_rocksmith.so: 5,376,000 bytes decoded (PCM16 confirmed, was 10,752,000 float32)
- godot_rocksmith.dll: rebuilt with same fix (was never updated after bb7ea19)

Verified on Linux sandbox:
  AudioEngine: decoded 496786 WEM bytes → 5376000 PCM bytes (2 ch, 48000 Hz)
  = 28s × 48000 × 2ch × 2 bytes (PCM16) ✓

fix: downgrade REQUEST_CHANGES to COMMENT on bot's own PRs (#51)

## Problem

PR #49 review failed with 422 — GitHub doesn't allow `REQUEST_CHANGES`
on your own PR. The bot created #49 via `/implement`, then tried to
review it with `REQUEST_CHANGES`.

## Fix

When reviewing a PR authored by the bot, downgrade `REQUEST_CHANGES` to
`COMMENT`. The feedback still gets posted, it just doesn't block.

commit.message:
fix: use MYSQL_PWD env var instead of .cnf file for MySQL passwords

MySQL .cnf files don't properly handle passwords with % characters
even when quoted, as % is treated as a variable placeholder.
Using MYSQL_PWD environment variable instead, which correctly
handles all special characters while still keeping the password
out of process args (ps aux).

Fixes: #issue-with-special-chars-in-mysql-passwords
